zones.label: Thonny
zones.summary: Best for beginners — one-click MicroPython setup
facts: MicroPython support: Built-in REPL; flash files directly to board; package installer
Key strength: One-click MicroPython board setup; no configuration needed
Best for: First-time coders, classroom instruction

zones.label: VS Code + MicroPico
zones.summary: Best for growing projects — professional workflow
facts: MicroPython support: MicroPico extension; REPL, upload/download, IntelliSense stubs
Key strength: Git integration, extensions, professional workflow
Best for: Larger projects; developers comfortable with tools

zones.label: Mu Editor
zones.summary: Simple and visual — built-in serial plotter
facts: MicroPython support: Built-in REPL; auto-detects BBC micro:bit and Pico
Key strength: Built-in serial plotter for sensor data; clean uncluttered UI
Best for: Students who want simplicity; sensor data visualization

zones.label: Wokwi Simulator
zones.summary: No hardware needed — simulate circuits in a browser
facts: Operating system: Any browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge)
MicroPython support: Full MicroPython simulation for Pico, ESP32; virtual sensors
Key strength: Simulate circuits online; shareable project links; no real hardware required
Best for: Learning without hardware; sharing demos; remote teaching

quiz.question: Which IDE lets you write and run MicroPython code in a browser with no real hardware?
quiz.explanation: Wokwi is a browser-based simulator that fully simulates MicroPython on the Pico and ESP32 with virtual sensors — perfect for learning without hardware, sharing demos, and remote teaching.

quiz.question: Which IDE is recommended for first-time coders because it sets up a MicroPython board with one click and needs no configuration?
quiz.explanation: Thonny is the beginner's choice: it has a built-in REPL, flashes files directly to the board, and configures a MicroPython board in one click with no setup needed.

quiz.question: Which IDE adds Git integration and a professional workflow for larger projects through the MicroPico extension?
quiz.explanation: VS Code with the MicroPico extension offers Git integration, IntelliSense stubs, full debugging, and extensions — ideal for larger projects and developers comfortable with professional tools.

quiz.question: Which IDE has a built-in serial plotter that graphs sensor data with a clean, uncluttered interface?
quiz.explanation: Mu Editor's standout feature is its built-in serial plotter for visualizing sensor data, paired with a clean, simple UI that auto-detects the BBC micro:bit and Pico.

quiz.question: You want step-by-step visual debugging while learning — which beginner IDE provides it?
quiz.explanation: Thonny includes a step-by-step visual debugger aimed at beginners, while Mu Editor has no debugger and VS Code's debugging comes through an extension.
